scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

Investigation notes (memory/escalation.md) show Acme's dashboard displays Feb 1 (billing period start) while Acme expects Jan 15 (actual signup). Engineering implemented the billing-period-start because the spec ("Display subscription start date") is ambiguous. The documented fix is a 1-line UI label change (e.g., "Billing period start") or showing both dates — no backend/data change required.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B) Annual billing requires proration, refund, and dunning changes that would conflict with the March 31 billing migration launch.

Explanation: Alan raised annual billing in the #leadership log (logs/2026-03-07.md). The Billing Migration is already targeting a March 31 launch with reconciliation of legacy subscriptions as a blocker and no buffer — slipping past March 15 causes a miss (memory/projects.md). Introducing annual billing would add billing logic changes (proration/refunds/dunning) that would interfere with that tight March 31 migration schedule.
